\boxed{C}
**Clinical Reasoning:** The retrieved evidence emphasizes that HIV sentinel surveillance involves epidemiological tracking of disease distribution, including incidence rates, risk patterns, and trends. Specifically, references indicate that surveillance enables "standardization of reporting," "identification of demographic trends," and "epidemiological tracking" — directly aligning with estimating disease incidence. While option A (monitoring disease trends) is partially supported, C best captures the core purpose of sentinel surveillance as quantified measurement of new cases over time.
